summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / anti-fascism, Democracy / press freedom, AI / privacy. The strongest items in this run are Trump news at a glance: another Trump cabinet member out – but not Kash Patel, Here’s how Amazon’s price fixing allegedly drove up prices everywhere, and Standard-Based Grading Offers a Different Model of Assessing Student Learning.

mainItems.title: Trump news at a glance: another Trump cabinet member out – but not Kash Patel
mainItems.summary: Labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer exits, while Patel sues over story citing sources that allege ‘conspicuous inebriation and unexplained absences’ by FBI director Donald Trump ’s labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is stepping down, the…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Here’s how Amazon’s price fixing allegedly drove up prices everywhere
mainItems.summary: On Monday, California Attorney General Rob Bonta revealed the evidence of alleged price-fixing by Amazon.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Standard-Based Grading Offers a Different Model of Assessing Student Learning
mainItems.summary: Some school districts, including ones in Maine, New Mexico, Iowa and Oregon, are shifting to standards-based grading, where students are graded on the skills and concepts they learn instead of points accumulated from assignments and tests…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: My family tried to eat fewer ultra-processed foods for five years. Here’s what we learned
mainItems.summary: Cutting UPFs from our grocery list was expensive, laborious and time-consuming Tell us: do you use AI for fitness? Grocery shopping looks different these days.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: The emotional security secret: how to get healthier, happier and have stronger relationships
mainItems.summary: Psychiatrist Amir Levine’s first book explored different types of attachment. In his follow up, he explains how anyone can become more secure Amir Levine has been quietly working towards a second book for 16 years.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Tim Cook will still be Apple’s Trump whisperer
mainItems.summary: Though Tim Cook is shedding his CEO title for the role of Apple's executive chairman, it appears he'll keep one of his most important duties: that of the company's Trump whisperer.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Trump signs memos to boost US fossil-fuel production for ‘defense readiness’
mainItems.summary: President claims ‘inadequate’ supply presents security threat and orders expansion of oil, coal and gas production Donald Trump on Monday released a series of memos that doubled down on his support of increased domestic fossil fuel…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: California Students Author New ‘Digital Wellness’ Bill, Say Phone Bans Fall Short
mainItems.summary: After taking a break from social media, Orange County student Elise Choi helped write a bill that would mandate California schools teach digital wellness — a response to growing concerns about how technology is affecting students’ mental…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: New ID@Xbox showcase in collaboration with IGN set for 23rd April
mainItems.summary: IGN is partnering with ID@Xbox again to deliver a new showcase on 23rd April , and we've received the first details about the games coming to Xbox and PC that will be present. Read more
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
mainItems.summary: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Scientists Gave a Bunch of Salmon Cocaine. This Is What Happened Next.
mainItems.summary: Salmon exposed to cocaine and its byproduct swam farther than unexposed fish, raising alarms about drug pollution in aquatic ecosystems.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Hokum Trailer Sees Adam Scott Surrounded By Dead Goats and an Evil Witch
mainItems.summary: A long time ago, there was a witch who scared the crap out of me in this trailer The post Hokum Trailer Sees Adam Scott Surrounded By Dead Goats and an Evil Witch appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
mainItems.summary: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
